changes.reason: The note says USP guidance was incorporated. Under the proposed policy, a completed abstracted reference review is labeled synthetic_reference_consulted unless the note explicitly states that the requested conclusion is unsupported.

before: Product-specific refrigerated stability is not supported by the public corpus.

changes.reason: Hospitalization appears in the complaint but not in the canonical reviewer note. The extraction contract treats severe_triggers_observed as reviewer-confirmed findings, so complaint-only facts are not gold labels for reviewer-note extraction.
The limitation now mirrors the supplied investigation instead of adding a public-corpus conclusion that the reviewer note did not state.

before: The complete proprietary inactive-ingredient formula is not supported by the public corpus.
after: The full proprietary formula was not disclosed.

changes.reason: The limitation now mirrors the supplied investigation and preserves the explicit non-disclosure boundary.

changes.reason: This is an adverse-event investigation, but the canonical reviewer note does not document record-review or lot-trend outcomes. Under the proposed silence policy, required-but-undocumented review maps to documentation_incomplete and unavailable rather than not_applicable.
This case includes a reported transdermal reaction and formulation concern. Record and lot review remain relevant, but their outcomes are not documented in the canonical investigation.
USP guidance was incorporated. The note does not explicitly state that the requested refrigerated-efficacy conclusion was unsupported.

before: Product-specific efficacy after refrigeration is not supported by the public corpus.
